description:
You are a backup administrator who wants to keep every-thing related to archiving jobs fully isolated from the system
    Python installation.  
    1. Inside your home directory create a new workspace directory named  
       /home/user/backups/scripts  
       (create the entire path if it does not already exist).  
    2. In that directory create a Python virtual environment called
       archive_env using the standard library’s venv module.  
       The final absolute path of the virtual-environment directory **must be**  
       /home/user/backups/scripts/archive_env  
       and, once created, it has to contain the default structure produced by 
       venv (e.g. the bin/, lib/, pyvenv.cfg files etc.).  
    3. After the virtual environment is successfully created, generate a plain-text
       log file located at  
       /home/user/backups/archive_env_setup.log  

       The required content and exact format of the log file is:  

       • Line 1 : the exact absolute path to the virtual-environment directory  
                  (/home/user/backups/scripts/archive_env)  
       • Line 2 : the literal word **VENV_READY** (all capitals, no extra spaces)  

       There must be **exactly two lines**, each terminated with a single newline
       character (UNIX line ending, “\n”).  
    4. Do not install any additional packages; the task is only to set up the
       virtual environment and create the log file.

    The automated tests will confirm all three of the following:
    a) that /home/user/backups/scripts/archive_env exists,  
    b) that its bin/activate script exists, proving venv was created, and  
    c) that /home/user/backups/archive_env_setup.log exists and matches the
       exact two-line format specified above.

    You do not need root privileges. Everything can be achieved with a single
    terminal command plus simple redirection or echo for the log file.
truth:
The grader will verify:

    1. Directory /home/user/backups/scripts/archive_env is present and is a directory.
    2. File /home/user/backups/scripts/archive_env/bin/activate is present and non-empty.
    3. File /home/user/backups/archive_env_setup.log exists and its exact
       contents (including the trailing newline after the second line) are:

       Line 1 : "/home/user/backups/scripts/archive_env\n"
       Line 2 : "VENV_READY\n"
